QF.id: VI Functions of DP Account Opening_21

question.text: For an illiterate person opening a demat account, what must the DP official do?

answer.text: Read out and explain the contents of the Rights and Obligations document and delivery instruction form to the client
Ask the person to bring a literate representative
Only obtain a thumb impression without any explanation

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The DP official must read out and explain the contents, then put their signature and remark 'Details explained to the Client(s)' on the forms. **The Trap:** 'Refuse to open the account' would be discriminatory — illiterate persons have every right to open demat accounts. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series V-A covers account opening for illiterate investors in mutual funds. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If the illiterate person is temporarily or permanently disabled and cannot come in person, thumb impressions on instruction forms must be attested.
